briefs.title: Council to consider Land Acknowledgement for Langley City meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will open its April 27, 2026 meeting with a Land Acknowledgement recognizing the traditional unceded territory of the Katzie, Kwantlen, Matsqui, and Semiahmoo First Nations. This is a ceremonial statement and does not propose any new rules, bylaws, or requirements for local businesses.

briefs.title: Council to observe April 28 Day of Mourning for workers killed or injured on the job
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will hold a moment of silence at its April 27, 2026 meeting to mark April 28 — Canada's National Day of Mourning. This day honours workers who have been killed, injured, or made ill because of their work. No new rules or requirements are being proposed; this is a symbolic recognition only.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proclaiming May 2026 as MS Awareness Month in Langley
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will consider a request to officially proclaim May 2026 as MS Awareness Month. The proposal also asks the City to fly the MS Canada flag outside City offices throughout May and to light up Douglas Park in red on May 30 for World MS Day.

key_dates.label: World MS Day — Douglas Park red illumination requested, Langley

briefs.title: BC Farmers Market Nutrition Coupon Program Receives $4.25M in Provincial Funding for 2026
briefs.summary: The BC Ministry of Health has confirmed that the BC Association of Farmers' Markets (BCAFM) will receive $4.25 million to keep the Farmers' Market Nutrition Coupon Program running in 2026. This program provides coupons to low-income residents to spend at farmers' markets, which directly supports local farmers and market vendors across BC.
briefs.action_required: Contact BCAFM at info@bcfarmersmarket.org or 604-734-9797 to find out how your market or business can participate.
briefs.industries: Agriculture, Grocery & Food Retail, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to consider BC Farmers Market Nutrition Coupon Program funding update — Langley City
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will consider correspondence at its April 27, 2026 meeting, including a response from the BC Ministry of Health confirming that $4.25 million has been provided to the BC Association of Farmers' Markets to keep the Farmers Market Nutrition Coupon Program running in 2026. The program helps lower-income families, seniors, and pregnant individuals buy healthy local produce using coupons redeemable at farmers markets — and it also puts money directly into local farmers' pockets.
briefs.action_required: If you sell at a farmers market, contact the BC Association of Farmers' Markets to confirm your market's participation in the 2026 program.

briefs.title: Council to vote on stricter rules for securing vacant buildings and lots in Langley
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would require owners of vacant properties — empty buildings or lots where no one legally lives or works — to both board up all openings AND install security fencing. Right now, only one of those measures is required. The proposed rules would also set out specific materials and methods for boarding and fencing, and could require extras like security cameras, alarms, or guards depending on site conditions.
briefs.action_required: If you own a vacant property in Langley, review the proposed bylaw now — new boarding and fencing rules may apply to you.

key_dates.label: Langley Community Standards Bylaw Amendment No. 3338 — final reading vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider Bylaws: Langley City — April 27, 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on one or more bylaws at its April 27, 2026 meeting. The agenda references bylaw items under section 9, but full details of what the bylaws cover have not been provided in this summary. Business owners in Langley should review the full agenda to find out if any proposed rules could affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Langley City Council agenda for April 27, 2026 to see if any bylaws affect your business.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council bylaw vote — council meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Approve April 13 Special Meeting Minutes — Langley City
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on adopting the official minutes from its April 13, 2026 special meeting. That meeting included a presentation on 'Project Black Feather,' a student support program run by School District No. 35, which has been formally evaluated and will continue with school district funding next year.

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving minutes from Langley City's April 7 closed meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on formally approving the minutes from a brief special meeting held on April 7, 2026. At that meeting, councillors voted to move into a closed session — meaning a private meeting not open to the public — to discuss a personnel matter and receive confidential legal advice.

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving April 13, 2026 Langley City Council meeting minutes
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on adopting the official minutes from its April 13, 2026 regular meeting. Those minutes record several decisions already made at that meeting, including first readings of bylaws on community standards and affordable housing, emergency preparedness grant applications, and motions on mental health response units, long-term care beds, and a future slow streets program.
briefs.action_required: Review the April 13 minutes if any decisions from that meeting affect your business.

key_dates.label: Langley City 2027 budget — slow streets program capital item consideration

briefs.title: Council to Consider Consent Agenda Items at Langley City Meeting — April 27, 2026
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on a group of routine items bundled together under a 'Consent Agenda' — a process where multiple items are approved in one motion unless a council member asks to pull one out for separate discussion. No specific business-impacting items are detailed in this notice, but any council member may request changes to what is included before the vote takes place.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Langley City Council agenda for April 27, 2026 to see if any items affect your business.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council Consent Agenda meeting date — April 27, 2026

briefs.title: Council to consider: Adoption of previous meeting minutes — Langley City
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on formally adopting the minutes from a previous meeting as part of the Consent Agenda. This is a routine administrative step — it simply confirms that the written record of what was discussed and decided at that earlier meeting is accurate.

briefs.title: Council to Consider RCMP Update for Langley City — April 2026
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to receive an update from Superintendent Harm Dosange, the Officer in Charge of Langley RCMP, at its April 27, 2026 meeting. This is an informational briefing on local policing matters — no specific bylaw or business regulation change has been detailed in the agenda item.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 27 council meeting for any policing changes that could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council RCMP briefing meeting date

briefs.title: Council to Consider RCMP Update at Langley City's April 27 Meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to receive a community spotlight update from the RCMP at its April 27, 2026 regular meeting. Superintendent Harm Dosange, Officer in Charge of Langley RCMP, will present the update to Council — this is an informational item, not a vote, but it gives the public a chance to hear about local policing matters.
briefs.action_required: Attend or watch the April 27 Council meeting livestream to hear the RCMP update.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council regular meeting — RCMP community spotlight presentation

briefs.title: Council to consider advisory body updates at April 27 Langley City meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will consider updates from its advisory bodies at the April 27, 2026 regular meeting. This agenda item gives Council members a chance to share news from committees they sit on — such as the Environmental Sustainability Committee, which recently highlighted its Earth Day event at Douglas Park.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Langley City Regular Meeting Dates for May 2026
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to hold two upcoming regular meetings on May 11 and May 25, 2026. These are scheduled sessions where council will consider local business and community matters — no specific decisions have been announced yet for these dates.
briefs.action_required: Check the Langley City Council agenda closer to each date to see if items affect your business.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council regular meeting — May 2026 (first session)
Langley City Council regular meeting — May 2026 (second session)

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley City Council to vote on tax rates, affordable housing fund, and contract transparency
briefs.summary: At its April 27, 2026 meeting, Langley City Council is set to vote on several significant items: a 2026 Tax Rate Bylaw that would raise property taxes by 5.82% to fund the approved city budget; final approval of an Affordable Housing Reserve Fund bylaw and related policy that would create a dedicated fund to help build below-market housing; and a motion that would require staff to always present a 'status quo' cost option alongside any proposed service contract changes so Council can compare true costs before deciding.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's April 27 decisions — the 5.82% property tax increase and new affordable housing fund rules could affect your costs.

key_dates.label: Langley 2026 Tax Rate Bylaw — Council vote (first, second, third reading)
Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Bylaw — final reading vote
Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Policy CO-89 — Council approval vote
Langley Community Standards Bylaw Amendment No. 3338 — final reading vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley City Council to vote on Affordable Housing Reserve Fund policy
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on a new policy called CO-89, which would set the rules for an Affordable Housing Reserve Fund — a dedicated pool of money used to support affordable housing projects. The policy would spell out how money gets deposited into the fund and how it can be spent.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development contributions or affordable housing projects in Langley.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council meeting — CO-89 Affordable Housing Reserve Fund policy vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Bylaw final approval
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on final approval of Bylaw 3320, which would create a dedicated savings account (called a reserve fund) to collect and hold money for building affordable housing. Under the proposed bylaw, developers who can't include affordable units in their projects would pay 'cash-in-lieu' — a fee paid instead — into this fund, which Council could then use to help build new below-market housing in partnership with non-profits and senior governments.
briefs.action_required: If you develop property in Langley, watch for this vote — it sets new fees tied to affordable housing requirements.

key_dates.label: Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Bylaw 3320 — final reading vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Langley City 2026 property tax rate bylaw
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on Bylaw No. 3336, which would set the property tax rates used to collect municipal taxes for 2026. If passed, this bylaw would determine how much property owners — including business owners — owe in city taxes this year.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley City 2026 Tax Rates and Affordable Housing Fund Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on April 27, 2026 on two key bylaws: a 2026 Tax Rate Bylaw that would set a 5.82% property tax increase to fund the city's approved budget, and an Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Bylaw that would create a dedicated savings account (reserve fund) to help build below-market housing in the city. Council will also consider a related policy guiding how that housing fund can be spent.
briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property tax bill — business and light industrial owners face increases averaging $1,643/year.

key_dates.label: Langley City 2026 Tax Rate Bylaw — Council vote (first, second, third reading)
Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Bylaw — final reading vote
Langley Affordable Housing Reserve Fund Policy CO-89 — Council approval vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley City Council to require cost comparisons for all future service contracts
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on a motion that would require city staff to always include a 'status quo' option — meaning a clear estimate of what it costs to keep things as they are — whenever a service contract is up for renewal. This would sit alongside any proposed changes, so Council can compare the two before making a decision.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this could affect how city service contracts that impact your business are evaluated.

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley City Council to vote on contract cost-benefit review policy
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a motion that would require city staff to always include a 'status quo' option — meaning a clear estimate of what it costs to keep things as they are — whenever a service contract is up for renewal. This would sit alongside any proposed changes, so Council can compare the full costs and service impacts before making a decision.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 27 — this could affect how the City evaluates future service contracts.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council regular meeting — cost-benefit contract policy vote

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Langley Council to vote on contract cost-benefit review policy
briefs.summary: At its April 27, 2026 meeting, Langley City Council is set to vote on a motion put forward by Councillor Solyom that would require city staff to always include a 'status quo' option — meaning a clear estimate of what it would cost to simply keep the current service arrangement — whenever Council considers renewing or changing a service contract. This 'apples-to-apples' comparison would sit alongside any proposed changes so Council can judge whether switching is actually worth it.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how the City evaluates contracts that involve local businesses.

key_dates.label: Langley City Council meeting — cost-benefit contract policy vote

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance
